analysis_methods: Two-proportion z tests for binary or threshold subgroup risk differences in objective_response.
Delta-method additive interaction contrasts for 2x2 subgroup risk differences.
HC1 robust linear probability models for adjusted effects on the objective_response probability scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_005` equal to 1 have higher mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_005` equal to 0.
Patients with `feature_034` equal to 1 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_034` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 36.1% for feature_005=1 versus 15.9% for feature_005=0; risk difference +20.2 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
Response was 15.4% for feature_034=1 versus 34.6% for feature_034=0; risk difference -19.2 percentage points, p=<1e-300.

iterations.update: The two largest binary main effects pointed in opposite directions, motivating interaction tests between them.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_065` equal to 1 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_065` equal to 0.
Patients with `feature_009` equal to 1 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_009` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 15.0% for feature_065=1 versus 25.0% for feature_065=0; risk difference -10.1 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
Response was 16.0% for feature_009=1 versus 26.0% for feature_009=0; risk difference -10.1 percentage points, p=<1e-300.

iterations.update: Two additional adverse binary features had very similar negative associations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_055` equal to 1 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_055` equal to 0.
Patients with `feature_067` equal to 1 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_067` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 17.6% for feature_055=1 versus 24.2% for feature_055=0; risk difference -6.6 percentage points, p=2.48e-9.
Response was 23.3% for feature_067=1 versus 24.2% for feature_067=0; risk difference -0.9 percentage points, p=0.072.

iterations.update: feature_055 was supported as a rarer adverse marker; feature_067 was not significant at 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with upper-quartile `feature_003` values (>= 41.28) have lower mean `objective_response` than all other patients.
Patients with lower-quartile `feature_003` values (<= 6.46) have higher mean `objective_response` than all other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 15.3% for feature_003>=41.28 versus 26.9% otherwise; risk difference -11.7 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
Response was 31.8% for feature_003<=6.46 versus 21.4% otherwise; risk difference +10.4 percentage points, p=<1e-300.

iterations.update: feature_003 showed a strong monotone-looking unfavorable pattern, so later iterations tested whether it modified the feature_005 subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with upper-quartile `feature_063` values (>= 6.4) have lower mean `objective_response` than all other patients.
Patients with `feature_063` equal to 0 have higher mean `objective_response` than patients with nonzero `feature_063`.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 21.1% for feature_063>=6.4 versus 25.0% otherwise; risk difference -3.9 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
Response was 26.3% for feature_063=0 versus 23.2% for nonzero feature_063; risk difference +3.1 percentage points, p=4.19e-13.

iterations.update: feature_063 added a second continuous adverse pattern, smaller than feature_003 but significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with upper-quartile `feature_054` values (>= 6.98) have lower mean `objective_response` than all other patients.
Patients with upper-quartile `feature_027` values (>= 4.1) have higher mean `objective_response` than all other patients.
Patients with lower-quartile `feature_027` values (<= 3.5) have lower mean `objective_response` than all other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.1% for feature_054>=6.98 versus 24.7% otherwise; risk difference -2.6 percentage points, p=7.14e-9.
Response was 24.9% for feature_027>=4.1 versus 23.6% otherwise; risk difference +1.3 percentage points, p=0.002.
Response was 23.1% for feature_027<=3.5 versus 24.4% otherwise; risk difference -1.4 percentage points, p=0.001.

iterations.update: feature_054 was adverse; feature_027 showed a smaller favorable high-end gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with upper-quartile `feature_039` values (>= 71.8) have higher mean `objective_response` than all other patients.
Patients with `feature_025` equal to 1 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_025` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 24.1% for feature_039>=71.8 versus 24.0% otherwise; risk difference +0.1 percentage points, p=0.750.
Response was 22.2% for feature_025=1 versus 24.1% for feature_025=0; risk difference -1.9 percentage points, p=0.097.

iterations.update: These checks supplied null or weak comparators: neither feature_039 nor feature_025 met p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_023` equal to 2 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_023` equal to 0.
Patients with `feature_040` equal to 10 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with `feature_040` equal to 6.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 18.2% for feature_023=2 versus 28.2% for feature_023=0; risk difference -10.0 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
Response was 24.2% for feature_040=10 versus 24.4% for feature_040=6; risk difference -0.1 percentage points, p=0.913.

iterations.update: One low-cardinality feature had a clear gradient, whereas another did not.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` equal to 1 and `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `feature_034` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_034` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_005 risk difference was +0.8 percentage points when feature_034=1 and +44.0 percentage points when feature_034=0; interaction contrast -43.3 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: feature_034 almost completely removed the feature_005 high-response pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_005` equal to 1 and `feature_034` equal to 0 have higher mean `objective_response` than all other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 61.0% for feature_005=1 and feature_034=0 versus 15.9% for all other patients; risk difference +45.1 percentage points, p=<1e-300.

iterations.update: The high-response subgroup was concentrated in the feature_005=1 and feature_034=0 combination.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` equal to 1 and `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `feature_009` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_009` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_005 risk difference was +0.8 percentage points when feature_009=1 and +25.1 percentage points when feature_009=0; interaction contrast -24.3 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: feature_009 also attenuated the feature_005 favorable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` equal to 1 and `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `feature_065` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_065` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_005 risk difference was +0.6 percentage points when feature_065=1 and +22.5 percentage points when feature_065=0; interaction contrast -21.8 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: feature_065 showed a similar but slightly smaller attenuation of the feature_005 association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_005` equal to 1 and `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `feature_055` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_055` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_005 risk difference was -2.2 percentage points when feature_055=1 and +20.9 percentage points when feature_055=0; interaction contrast -23.1 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: The rare feature_055 subgroup again weakened the feature_005 signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between `feature_009` equal to 1 and `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `feature_034` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_034` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_009 risk difference was -0.1 percentage points when feature_034=1 and -22.2 percentage points when feature_034=0; interaction contrast +22.1 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: feature_009 was mainly adverse in feature_034=0 patients; feature_034=1 patients were already low-response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between `feature_065` equal to 1 and `objective_response` is smaller among patients with `feature_034` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_034` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_065 risk difference was -0.6 percentage points when feature_034=1 and -21.6 percentage points when feature_034=0; interaction contrast +21.0 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: feature_065 had the same attenuation pattern in feature_034=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_065` equal to 1 and `feature_009` equal to 1 has a less negative association with `objective_response` than expected from adding their separate negative risk differences.

analyses.result_summary: The additive interaction contrast for feature_065 and feature_009 was +12.6 percentage points on the response-probability scale, p=<1e-300; the double-positive group responded at 16.0%.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: The two adverse markers overlapped nonadditively: their combined decrement was not the sum of both single-marker decrements.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between upper-quartile `feature_003` and `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_005` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_005` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Upper-quartile feature_003 changed response by -25.0 percentage points when feature_005=1 and -2.7 percentage points when feature_005=0; interaction contrast -22.4 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: High feature_003 substantially eroded the otherwise favorable feature_005 subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between upper-quartile `feature_003` and `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_034` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_034` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Upper-quartile feature_003 changed response by -2.2 percentage points when feature_034=1 and -3.5 percentage points when feature_034=0; interaction contrast +1.3 percentage points, p=0.437.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: The feature_003 high effect did not materially differ by feature_034 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between upper-quartile `feature_063` and `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_005` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_005` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Upper-quartile feature_063 changed response by -4.4 percentage points when feature_005=1 and -3.4 percentage points when feature_005=0; interaction contrast -1.0 percentage points, p=0.273.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: Unlike feature_003, feature_063 did not significantly modify the feature_005 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `feature_057` equal to 1, `feature_048` equal to 1 is associated with higher `objective_response` than `feature_048` equal to 0, more so than among patients with `feature_057` equal to 0.

Among patients with `feature_053` equal to 1, `feature_048` equal to 1 is associated with higher `objective_response` than `feature_048` equal to 0, more so than among patients with `feature_053` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: The feature_048 by feature_057 interaction contrast was +7.3 percentage points, p=0.009; the double-positive response rate was 29.5%.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

analyses.result_summary: The feature_048 by feature_053 interaction contrast was +6.3 percentage points, p=0.009; the double-positive response rate was 28.5%.
analyses.test: delta-method difference-in-differences on objective_response risk differences

iterations.update: Two smaller interactions were statistically significant but involved smaller double-positive subgroups, so they are secondary findings.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with any of `feature_009`, `feature_065`, or `feature_055` equal to 1 have lower mean `objective_response` than patients with all three equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 15.7% for patients with any of feature_009/feature_065/feature_055 equal to 1 versus 27.6% for patients with all three equal to 0; risk difference -11.9 percentage points, p=<1e-300.

iterations.update: The three adverse binary markers form a useful composite low-response group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both upper-quartile `feature_003` (>= 41.28) and upper-quartile `feature_063` (>= 6.4) have lower mean `objective_response` than all other patients.
Patients with lower-quartile `feature_003` (<= 6.46) and `feature_063` equal to 0 have higher mean `objective_response` than all other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 11.9% for the feature_003-high/feature_063-high group versus 24.8% for others; risk difference -12.9 percentage points, p=<1e-300.
Response was 35.2% for feature_003<=6.46 and feature_063=0 versus 23.2% for others; risk difference +12.0 percentage points, p=<1e-300.

iterations.update: The continuous features combine into high- and low-response strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_005` equal to 1, `feature_034` equal to 0, and all of `feature_009`, `feature_065`, and `feature_055` equal to 0 have higher mean `objective_response` than all other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 79.8% for feature_005=1, feature_034=0, and no feature_009/feature_065/feature_055 flags versus 15.9% for all other patients; risk difference +63.8 percentage points, p=<1e-300.

iterations.update: Combining favorable feature_005/feature_034 status with absence of adverse flags isolated the highest-response group found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for `feature_034`, `feature_009`, `feature_065`, `feature_055`, standardized `feature_003`, standardized `feature_063`, standardized `feature_054`, standardized `feature_027`, and standardized `feature_039`, `feature_005` remains positively associated with `objective_response`.

After adjustment for `feature_005`, `feature_009`, `feature_065`, `feature_055`, standardized `feature_003`, standardized `feature_063`, standardized `feature_054`, standardized `feature_027`, and standardized `feature_039`, `feature_034` remains negatively associated with `objective_response`.
After adjustment for the leading binary features and standardized continuous features, standardized `feature_039` is positively associated with `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear-probability coefficient for feature_005 was +20.2 percentage points, robust p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: HC1 robust linear probability model coefficient

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear-probability coefficient for feature_034 was -18.8 percentage points, robust p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: HC1 robust linear probability model coefficient

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted per-SD coefficient for feature_039 was +0.1 percentage points, robust p=0.636.
analyses.test: HC1 robust linear probability model coefficient

iterations.update: The main feature_005 and feature_034 associations persisted after adjustment; feature_039 remained null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for leading main effects and continuous covariates, the `feature_005` by `feature_034` interaction remains negative for `objective_response`.
After adjustment for leading main effects and continuous covariates, the `feature_005` by `feature_009` interaction remains negative for `objective_response`.
After adjustment for leading main effects and continuous covariates, standardized `feature_003` remains negatively associated with `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient for feature_005:feature_034 was -43.1 percentage points, robust p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: HC1 robust linear probability model coefficient

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient for feature_005:feature_009 was -24.2 percentage points, robust p=<1e-300.
analyses.test: HC1 robust linear probability model coefficient

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted per-SD coefficient for feature_003 was -0.9 percentage points, robust p=3.60e-11.
analyses.test: HC1 robust linear probability model coefficient

iterations.update: The strongest interaction findings were not explained by the measured main effects or continuous covariates.
